release_title: GoalOS AGIALPHA Ascension AGI Jobs v0 (v2) ✨
tagline: Every mission becomes a market. Every market becomes proof. Every proof becomes accountable value.
description: A GoalOS-native constitutional economy for machine intelligence: compose bounded work, discover opportunities, run sealed institutional bids, form proof-producing coalitions, preserve independent validation and dissent, compile settlement evidence, and carry only human-authorized capability into institutional memory.

origin.translation: Original AGI Jobs v0 (v2) identity, staking, bidding, orchestration, validation, dispute, thermodynamic, attestation, certificate, reputation, and owner-control concepts translated into a browser-local GoalOS constitutional labor economy.

thesis.title: Work is a constitutional object
thesis.detail: Every job begins as a bounded mission contract with outcomes, evidence floors, resource envelopes, forbidden actions, dispute rights, and a named human authority.

thesis.title: Markets select coalitions, not slogans
thesis.detail: Twelve declared institutions submit deterministic sealed bids and form a prime coalition, specialist seats, a shadow council, and reserves.

thesis.title: Opportunity discovery precedes execution
thesis.detail: The exchange makes the need, capability gap, expected proof objects, and counterfactual alternatives explicit before work begins.

thesis.title: Execution must carry proof
thesis.detail: Work graphs, traces, energy ledgers, claims, contradictions, evaluator reports, and commitments form a twenty-four-artifact chain.

thesis.title: Validation resists capture
thesis.detail: Seven independent validator seats, five guardians, anti-collusion signals, preserved dissent, and fail-closed incidents stand between output and settlement review.

thesis.title: Economics remain subordinate to evidence
thesis.detail: A transparent six-part settlement waterfall is computed only after proof gates pass; the browser cannot connect a wallet or move value.

thesis.title: Memory is earned and reversible
thesis.detail: Capability and reputation updates remain scoped, expiring, revocable candidates until a human authority accepts the evidence package.

presets.name: Regional cold-chain resilience
presets.mission: Produce an evidence-grounded regional cold-chain resilience and energy continuity program for twelve public distribution hubs.

presets.name: Public procurement evidence review
presets.mission: Compare three public technology procurement strategies and produce a claim-by-claim evidence dossier with explicit uncertainty, authority, and conflict-of-interest controls.

presets.name: Manufacturing energy optimization
presets.mission: Design a bounded energy-efficiency opportunity map for a multi-site manufacturing network using declared assumptions, thermodynamic constraints, and reviewable evidence.

presets.name: Defensive software assurance
presets.mission: Review a fictional critical service architecture for defensive reliability gaps and produce a prioritized remediation plan without exploit development.

presets.name: Strategic market intelligence
presets.mission: Construct a source-bounded market landscape, competing theses, falsifiers, and strategic options for an emerging industrial category.

presets.name: Institutional knowledge artifact
presets.mission: Transform a fictional research archive into a decision-ready knowledge artifact with provenance, open questions, reusable evaluation, and revocation rules.

presets.name: Urban infrastructure coordination
presets.mission: Coordinate a bounded multi-institution planning exercise for resilient public infrastructure while preserving jurisdiction, dissent, and human approval.

presets.name: Scientific program design
presets.mission: Design a falsifiable multi-stage research program with hypotheses, evaluation gates, evidence standards, resource budgets, and stopping conditions.

postures.name: Evidence First
postures.description: Maximize provenance, validation depth, replayability, and uncertainty disclosure.

postures.name: Constitutional
postures.description: Maximize authority separation, identity integrity, dissent sensitivity, and fail-closed safety.

postures.name: Utility Balanced
postures.description: Balance outcome quality, evidence, cost discipline, delivery, safety, and energy.

postures.name: Speed Aware
postures.description: Favor bounded latency while retaining minimum proof, reliability, and authority floors.

postures.name: Planetary Efficient
postures.description: Optimize evidence yield and useful work per declared energy unit without weakening safety.

lifecycle.label: CHARTER
lifecycle.title: Mission contract sealed
lifecycle.detail: Outcome, evidence, resources, authority, disputes, and forbidden actions become a signed run commitment.

lifecycle.label: IDENTITY
lifecycle.title: Institutions admitted
lifecycle.detail: Synthetic identities, capabilities, credentials, conflicts, and revocation posture are checked before market entry.

lifecycle.label: OPPORTUNITY
lifecycle.title: Opportunity thesis committed
lifecycle.detail: The need, capability gap, counterfactual alternatives, expected proof objects, and failure conditions become explicit.

lifecycle.label: ESCROW
lifecycle.title: Settlement envelope reserved
lifecycle.detail: A local policy model reserves execution, validation, proof, dispute, resilience, and operations allocations without moving funds.

lifecycle.label: COMMIT
lifecycle.title: Sealed bids committed
lifecycle.detail: Candidate institutions commit capability, evidence, latency, resource, energy, and risk envelopes before rankings are visible.

lifecycle.label: REVEAL
lifecycle.title: Bids revealed and audited
lifecycle.detail: Commitments are deterministically revealed, scored, and checked for identity drift, capability inflation, and collusion signals.

lifecycle.label: COALITION
lifecycle.title: Execution coalition selected
lifecycle.detail: A prime institution, two specialists, a shadow council, and reserves are selected with a reviewable rationale.

lifecycle.label: GRAPH
lifecycle.title: Proof-producing work graph compiled
lifecycle.detail: The mission becomes bounded work packages with owners, dependencies, evidence objects, resource ceilings, and stop conditions.

lifecycle.label: EXECUTE
lifecycle.title: Bounded work executed
lifecycle.detail: A deterministic browser-local simulation emits work traces; no model, external tool, wallet, or network call occurs.

lifecycle.label: PROOF
lifecycle.title: Evidence chain sealed
lifecycle.detail: Sources, claims, contradictions, evaluations, energy, commitments, guardians, and settlement records receive SHA-256 commitments.

lifecycle.label: VALIDATE
lifecycle.title: Validator parliament revealed
lifecycle.detail: Seven separated seats reveal judgments; dissent remains visible and quorum is evaluated against the declared risk class.

lifecycle.label: CHALLENGE
lifecycle.title: Guardian challenge resolved
lifecycle.detail: Five guardians evaluate identity, proof, energy, settlement, and human-authority boundaries; material failures force SAFE_HOLD.

lifecycle.label: SETTLE
lifecycle.title: Settlement packet compiled
lifecycle.detail: A transparent allocation and contribution ledger is prepared as review evidence, never as a token transfer or payment authorization.

lifecycle.label: HUMAN
lifecycle.title: Human settlement and memory review required
lifecycle.detail: Only a human authority may approve external action, economic settlement, capability certification, or institutional-memory updates.

institutions.name: Atlas Evidence Guild
institutions.doctrine: Every consequential claim retains source identity, support scope, uncertainty, and a falsifier.

institutions.name: Meridian Systems Foundry
institutions.doctrine: Complex missions become explicit systems, dependencies, constraints, reversible interventions, and measured outcomes.

institutions.name: Concord Governance House
institutions.doctrine: Authority, jurisdiction, conflicts, dissent, and appeal rights precede optimization.

institutions.name: Forge Operations Collective
institutions.doctrine: Every plan must name owners, dependencies, resource limits, checkpoints, and recovery paths.

institutions.name: Aegis Assurance Chamber
institutions.doctrine: Unverified capability is a liability; safety, adversarial review, and fail-closed gates are productive work.

institutions.name: Lumina Knowledge Atelier
institutions.doctrine: Knowledge becomes institutional only when it is legible, attributable, testable, and revisable.

institutions.name: Polaris Planning Bureau
institutions.doctrine: Plans are portfolios of options with triggers, reversibility, and bounded commitments.

institutions.name: Verdant Efficiency Institute
institutions.doctrine: Useful work must be measured against energy, material, time, and counterfactual opportunity cost.

institutions.name: Sable Adversarial Laboratory
institutions.doctrine: Every confident plan deserves a hostile counter-model and explicit stop condition.

institutions.name: Orpheus Coordination Mesh
institutions.doctrine: Institutions collaborate through explicit interfaces, commitments, escalation paths, and shared proof.

institutions.name: Helios Energy Commons
institutions.doctrine: Thermodynamic constraints are constitutional limits, not post-hoc reporting fields.

institutions.name: Mnemosyne Memory Institute
institutions.doctrine: Capability memory must be scoped, expiring, evidence-bound, revocable, and never self-authorizing.

validators.name: Provenance Seat
validators.domain: source identity, support scope, citation and artifact lineage

validators.name: Outcome Seat
validators.domain: deliverable completeness, quality, utility, and counterfactual comparison

validators.name: Safety Seat
validators.domain: risk, forbidden actions, stop conditions, and fail-closed behavior

validators.name: Resource Seat
validators.domain: budget, efficiency, latency, energy, and resource-envelope adherence

validators.name: Identity Seat
validators.domain: institution identity, credential scope, conflict, and revocation posture

validators.name: Anti-Collusion Seat
validators.domain: commit/reveal integrity, correlated voting, capture signals, and minority protection

validators.name: Authority Seat
validators.domain: human control, settlement limits, legal reliance, production and external-action boundaries

guardians.name: Energy Guardian
guardians.domain: thermodynamic budget, efficiency floor, resource escalation

guardians.name: Settlement Guardian
guardians.domain: premature payout, reserve integrity, dispute and allocation boundaries

guardians.name: Human Authority Guardian
guardians.domain: production, funds, legal reliance, external action and memory promotion

guardrails.title: No wallet surface
guardrails.detail: The public experience contains no wallet provider, signing, token approval, or transaction path.

guardrails.title: No network runtime
guardrails.detail: No API, RPC, ENS, IPFS, model, telemetry, or external resource is requested at runtime.

guardrails.title: No autonomous settlement
guardrails.detail: All economic output is a demonstration allocation packet ending at human settlement review.

guardrails.title: Sealed-bid replayability
guardrails.detail: Bid commitments and reveals are deterministic, inspectable, and recomputable from the declared charter.

guardrails.title: Anti-collusion challenge
guardrails.detail: Correlated vote or bid signals are visible and can force SAFE_HOLD before settlement review.

guardrails.title: Thermodynamic ceiling
guardrails.detail: Energy and useful-work ratios remain explicit constitutional constraints.

guardrails.title: Dissent permanence
guardrails.detail: Minority validator judgment remains in the Evidence Docket and cannot be optimized away.

guardrails.title: Reversible memory
guardrails.detail: Certificates and reputation are only candidates with scope, expiry, revocation, and human approval.

guardrails.title: Canonical preservation
guardrails.detail: The feature is generated additively; canonical v86 source and unrelated outputs are hash-protected.

guardrails.title: Python 3.11 preflight
guardrails.detail: Every integrated Python tool compiles under the workflow runtime before the site build starts.

modules.name: Mission Charter Registry
modules.responsibility: Binds outcome, proof, resources, authority, disputes, and forbidden actions.

modules.name: Institution Admission Authority
modules.responsibility: Records synthetic identity, capability, conflicts, credentials, and revocation posture.

modules.name: Opportunity Discovery Engine
modules.responsibility: Frames capability gaps, counterfactuals, expected proof objects, and failure conditions.

modules.name: Settlement Envelope Compiler
modules.responsibility: Reserves six policy allocations without wallet connection or live value movement.

modules.name: Sealed Bid Commitment Book
modules.responsibility: Commits institutional capability, evidence, latency, resource, energy, and risk envelopes.

modules.name: Reveal & Pareto Selection Engine
modules.responsibility: Audits bid reveals, ranks institutions, computes a frontier, and identifies capture signals.

modules.name: Execution Coalition Foundry
modules.responsibility: Selects a prime, specialists, shadow council, and reserves with explicit rationale.

modules.name: Proof-Producing Work Graph
modules.responsibility: Compiles bounded work packages, dependencies, owners, evidence objects, and stop conditions.

modules.name: Bounded Runtime Theatre
modules.responsibility: Simulates execution locally and emits deterministic phase and resource traces.

modules.name: Evidence Chronicle
modules.responsibility: Chains twenty-four artifacts from charter to executive review with SHA-256 commitments.

modules.name: Validator Parliament
modules.responsibility: Runs seven independent commit/reveal seats and preserves dissent.

modules.name: Guardian & Dispute Chamber
modules.responsibility: Evaluates identity, evidence, energy, settlement, collusion, and authority incidents.

modules.name: Proof Settlement Chamber
modules.responsibility: Compiles allocation, contribution, reserve, and non-authorization records.

modules.name: Capability Memory Gate
modules.responsibility: Creates scoped, expiring, revocable memory candidates for human acceptance or rejection.

artifacts.name: Job Charter
artifacts.purpose: Mission, outcomes, deliverables, resources, risk, authority, disputes, and forbidden actions.

artifacts.name: Identity Admission Record
artifacts.purpose: Institution identities, credentials, capabilities, conflicts, and revocation posture.

artifacts.name: Opportunity Thesis
artifacts.purpose: Capability gap, counterfactual alternatives, expected proof objects, and failure conditions.

artifacts.name: Settlement Envelope
artifacts.purpose: Execution, validation, proof, dispute, resilience, and operations boundaries.

artifacts.name: Bid Commitments
artifacts.purpose: Sealed commitments for capability, evidence, latency, resources, energy, and risk.

artifacts.name: Bid Reveals
artifacts.purpose: Revealed institutional envelopes and commitment integrity results.

artifacts.name: Coalition Decision
artifacts.purpose: Prime, specialists, reserves, selection rationale, and weighted utility.

artifacts.name: Shadow Council Record
artifacts.purpose: Independent counterfactual institution and disagreement surface.

artifacts.name: Work Graph
artifacts.purpose: Proof-producing work packages, dependencies, owners, evidence, and stop conditions.

artifacts.name: Execution Trace
artifacts.purpose: Deterministic local phase transitions and bounded runtime record.

artifacts.name: Resource & Energy Ledger
artifacts.purpose: Useful-work, latency, resource, energy, ceiling, and efficiency record.

artifacts.name: Source Register
artifacts.purpose: Declared evidence identities, support scope, provenance class, and uncertainty.

artifacts.name: Claim Matrix
artifacts.purpose: Claims, evidence links, confidence, assumptions, and unresolved questions.

artifacts.name: Contradiction Register
artifacts.purpose: Competing evidence, objections, tensions, and disposition.

artifacts.name: Evaluator Report
artifacts.purpose: Capability, evidence, reliability, efficiency, latency, safety, energy, and utility.

artifacts.name: Validator Commitments
artifacts.purpose: Pre-reveal commitments for seven independent validator seats.

artifacts.name: Validator Reveals
artifacts.purpose: Judgments, scores, rationale, quorum, and preserved dissent.

artifacts.name: Dissent Memorandum
artifacts.purpose: Minority judgment and conditions that block stronger claims.

artifacts.name: Anti-Collusion Report
artifacts.purpose: Vote and bid correlation signals, randomness posture, and capture disposition.

artifacts.name: Guardian Disposition
artifacts.purpose: Identity, evidence, energy, settlement, and human-authority guardian decisions.

artifacts.name: Dispute Disposition
artifacts.purpose: Incident review, safe-hold decision, remediation, and appeal posture.

artifacts.name: Settlement Waterfall
artifacts.purpose: Transparent demonstration allocation and explicit non-authorization.

artifacts.name: Capability Memory Candidate
artifacts.purpose: Scope, evidence chain, expiry, decay, revocation, and human approval status.

artifacts.name: Executive Review Brief
artifacts.purpose: Decision-ready summary with settlement, production, funds, and external authority withheld.

architecture_translation.translation: Lifecycle calls become an evidence-bearing constitutional work state machine.
Synthetic identities and credentials are recorded without live ENS or wallet interaction.
Needs, capability gaps, counterfactuals, and proof requirements become explicit before bidding.
Stake and reward mechanics become a transparent local policy model with no live funds path.
Institutional bids are committed, revealed, audited, and ranked deterministically.
Agents become bounded institutions that form councils and emit proof-producing work graphs.
Execution is a browser-local deterministic simulation with explicit stop conditions.
Energy ceilings and useful-work ratios become governance gates, not optional telemetry.
Commit/reveal validation becomes seven separated evidence seats with risk-adjusted quorum.
Randomness posture and correlated behavior become inspectable evidence and safe-hold triggers.
Challenges, incidents, and appeals force remediation or SAFE_HOLD before settlement review.
Economic splits remain explicit policy evidence and cannot move assets from the browser.
Reputation updates require sealed evidence, scope, expiry, decay, revocation, and human approval.
Certificates remain review candidates with scope, evidence, expiry, lineage, and revocation.
Material identity, proof, quorum, collusion, energy, or authority breaches halt the market.
A non-technical user can compose and inspect work while CI compiles, verifies, browser-tests, preserves, and deploys.

governance_principles.title: Authority separation
governance_principles.detail: Employer intent, opportunity analysis, bidding, execution, validation, challenge, settlement preparation, memory, and final authorization remain distinct.

governance_principles.title: Least authority
governance_principles.detail: Every institution receives only the scope and capabilities needed for its declared work package.

governance_principles.title: Commit before reveal
governance_principles.detail: Institutional bids and validator judgments cannot adapt opportunistically after competitors are visible.

governance_principles.title: Evidence before economics
governance_principles.detail: No settlement packet is compiled until the proof chain, quorum, and guardian thresholds are complete.

governance_principles.title: Dissent by construction
governance_principles.detail: Minority judgment is governance evidence, not an optimization error.

governance_principles.title: Thermodynamic honesty
governance_principles.detail: Energy, resource, latency, and useful-work ratios remain explicit and reviewable.

governance_principles.title: Fail-closed challenge
governance_principles.detail: Material identity, evidence, quorum, collusion, energy, or authority failures produce SAFE_HOLD.

governance_principles.title: Replayable markets
governance_principles.detail: Weights, bids, frontier, coalition, shadow council, and contributions remain deterministic and inspectable.

governance_principles.title: Reversible memory
governance_principles.detail: Reputation and capability candidates remain scoped, expiring, decaying, revocable, and subordinate to later evidence.

governance_principles.title: Human economic authority
governance_principles.detail: The browser may prepare a packet; it cannot authorize production, payment, legal reliance, memory promotion, or external action.

threats.title: Identity spoofing
threats.control: Identity admission, role separation, revocation posture, and guardian veto.

threats.title: Capability inflation
threats.control: Sealed bids, evidence-weighted matching, Pareto frontier, and shadow council.

threats.title: Citation or artifact fabrication
threats.control: Artifact hashes, source register, claim matrix, contradiction record, and replay.

threats.title: Validator collusion
threats.control: Commit/reveal seats, correlation analysis, anti-collusion validator, guardian challenge, and dissent.

threats.title: Bid cartel formation
threats.control: Commitment integrity, ranking diversity, specialist separation, and shadow comparison.

threats.title: Budget capture
threats.control: Settlement envelope, contribution ledger, allocation caps, and budget incident rehearsal.

threats.title: Energy externalization
threats.control: Thermodynamic ceiling, energy guardian, useful-work ratio, and resilience reserve.

threats.title: Dispute suppression
threats.control: Dedicated challenge chamber, preserved objections, appeal posture, and reserve.

threats.title: Certificate laundering
threats.control: Candidate-only status, scope, expiry, decay, lineage, and human approval.

memory_rules.title: Scope
memory_rules.detail: A memory candidate applies only to the declared job class, deliverables, and evidence chain.

memory_rules.title: Expiry
memory_rules.detail: Every candidate carries a review date and cannot become permanent by default.

memory_rules.title: Decay
memory_rules.detail: Confidence decays without fresh evidence, repeated evaluation, and continued identity integrity.

memory_rules.title: Revocation
memory_rules.detail: Later contradiction, dispute, identity failure, or safety incident can revoke the candidate.

memory_rules.title: Dissent retention
memory_rules.detail: Minority validator judgment remains attached to the memory object.

memory_rules.title: No transitive authority
memory_rules.detail: A capability candidate does not imply legal, production, financial, or cross-domain authority.

memory_rules.title: Human acceptance
memory_rules.detail: Only an accountable human authority may promote a candidate into institutional memory.

memory_rules.title: Replayability
memory_rules.detail: The source docket, chain head, selection rationale, and evaluation remain reconstructable.

economic_invariants.title: No proof, no settlement packet
economic_invariants.detail: The settlement compiler remains closed until the full evidence chain is sealed.

economic_invariants.title: No quorum, no settlement packet
economic_invariants.detail: Risk-adjusted validator quorum is mandatory and cannot be bypassed by the prime coalition.

economic_invariants.title: Guardian veto survives majority
economic_invariants.detail: Material constitutional incidents can stop settlement even with a numerical validator majority.

economic_invariants.title: Dispute reserve is explicit
economic_invariants.detail: Challenge capacity is funded in the model before execution, not improvised after failure.

economic_invariants.title: Energy has a price signal
economic_invariants.detail: The allocation model exposes energy and resilience as explicit bounded costs.

economic_invariants.title: Contributions are attributable
economic_invariants.detail: Execution, specialist, validation, proof, dispute, and operations contributions remain inspectable.

economic_invariants.title: Live value movement is impossible
economic_invariants.detail: The browser exposes no wallet, provider, signer, token contract, or transaction path.

economic_invariants.title: Human authorization is final
economic_invariants.detail: Economic settlement remains a review packet until an external accountable process approves it.

claim_boundary: This is a deterministic browser-local protocol demonstration, not a live labor marketplace.
It does not claim achieved AGI, ASI, empirical superiority, production readiness, or real-world economic performance.
Institution identities, opportunities, bids, coalitions, work traces, validator judgments, guardians, reputation, certificates, energy, and economics are synthetic demonstration records.
No model, external tool, wallet, token contract, RPC endpoint, API, ENS resolver, IPFS client, telemetry endpoint, or blockchain transaction is used at runtime.
The GoalOS Ethereum Mainnet record is infrastructure context only and does not establish deployment of AGI Jobs v0 (v2).
Factual correctness of external claims is not certified by the browser experience.
No user funds, production activation, legal reliance, settlement, capability promotion, or external action is authorized.
Every successful run terminates at a human authority boundary; material incidents fail closed.
Source lineage fingerprints prove translation inputs, not correctness, security, audit completion, or product-market validation.
